mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers short-term discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days when the decline is part of a genuine easing cycle rather than a one-off noise move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months where FEDFUNDS trends down for at least 2 consecutive readings, or if the rolling 3-year sign accuracy falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An above-trend CPIAUCSL reading paired with FEDFUNDS below the inflation rate implies negative real rates and easier real financing conditions, which should support equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annualized inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not show higher average 20-day SPX returns than untriggered periods, or if the interaction term adds no out-of-sample lift versus FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and future demand, so a monthly increase in unemployment should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points from the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if months with rising UNRATE still produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the effect sign flips in a rolling 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending and stronger near-term revenue growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon through upward earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS growth does not coincide with above-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the relationship is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST is also improving, firms are expanding output and construction demand is firm, a combination that should forecast positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through cyclical earnings strength.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both post positive 3-month changes
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in INDPRO and HOUST fail to beat the unconditional 60-day return distribution, or if either series alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 growth after a prior expansion phase marks demand deceleration, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as analysts cut revenue and profit forecasts.
candidate_trigger: quarterly GDPC1 growth turns negative versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal disappears after accounting for lagged market returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market deterioration that weakens consumption and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through immediate earnings-risk repricing.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release cycle
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor shock does not lead to lower 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if only one of the two proxies carries the full predictive content.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-tightening stabilization phase, CPIAUCSL deceleration combined with FEDFUNDS still elevated indicates inflation relief before policy easing, which should be bullish for S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as discount-rate pressure fades.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation deceleration with still-high FEDFUNDS does not outperform the unconditional 60-day return distribution, or if the effect is absent once the CPI trend is removed.
